findings.reachability: Exploitable by an attacker who can control the Omnisend API response (compromised API key, MITM on the HTTPS call, or a rogue Omnisend-side contact record). The contactID from the API JSON response is cast to string and passed directly to PHP setcookie() with no newline stripping or sanitization.
findings.justification: In class-sync.php line 102-109, the API response contactID is extracted as `(string) $arr['contactID']` and returned; at line 28 it is passed to `Snippet::set_contact_cookie_id()`, which at class-snippet.php line 25 calls `setcookie('omnisendContactID', $contact_id, ...)` with no sanitization. PHP versions before 7.4 do not reject \r\n in cookie values, enabling a MITM attacker who injects newlines into contactID to split the HTTP response and inject arbitrary headers or a second response body.

detection_pattern.section_name: Cookie header injection: unsanitized value in setcookie()
detection_pattern.description: Finds setcookie() calls where the value argument is a variable. Without newline sanitization, an externally-controlled value can inject additional HTTP headers (response splitting). Anti-pattern checks for sanitization immediately preceding the call.

findings.reachability: Triggered automatically on every user registration (user_register hook), login (wp_login hook), profile update (profile_update hook), and batch cron sync. All WordPress users including administrators are affected. No authentication or capability check gates this path.
findings.justification: Line 88 reads `wp_capabilities` from user meta and lines 90-99 parse each active role (e.g. 'administrator', 'editor', 'shop_manager') into `$parsed_roles`, then line 98 transmits them to the Omnisend external API via `add_custom_property('wordpress_roles', ...)`. This exfiltrates user privilege information to a third-party SaaS for every synced WordPress user, including admins, with no redaction or user consent mechanism in the plugin.

detection_pattern.section_name: Sensitive data exfiltration: WordPress roles sent to third-party API
detection_pattern.description: Finds code that reads wp_capabilities user meta. When the resulting role array is forwarded to an external service without redaction, it leaks administrative privilege structure of the site's user base.
